claimMap.claim: v9.2: Human low-back-pain data used 240 mg salicin/day; salicylate safety cautions are essential.
claimMap.notes: v9.2: Human low-back-pain data used 240 mg salicin/day; salicylate safety cautions are essential.

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 11752510. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 11752510. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Aspirin or salicylate allergy or NSAID hypersensitivity (salicin is metabolized to salicylic acid and shares cross-reactive risk)
children or teenagers with a current or recent viral infection (theoretical Reye syndrome risk similar to aspirin)
bleeding disorders or anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy without clinician review
active GI ulcer or reflux disease
asthma with known aspirin or NSAID sensitivity
May increase bleeding risk with anticoagulants or antiplatelets or NSAIDs
may reduce the effectiveness of ACE inhibitors or diuretics
may increase methotrexate toxicity at high doses
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance.
